description: Bolivian drug cartel founded in the 1970s by Roberto Suárez Gómez, known as the 'King of Cocaine'. It became one of the world's largest cocaine producers after the 1980 coup, receiving political protection from the military dictatorship. The cartel supplied the Medellín Cartel and was active until the early 1990s.

country_links.evidence_quote: Drugs were smuggled from the cocaine labs in the Bolivian Amazon to Colombia, selling at $9,000 per kilo.
country_links.context: The organization smuggled drugs from Bolivia to Colombia and was a major supplier to the Medellín Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: who continued to smuggle drugs into the United States and began a major drug-trafficking operation in Southern California until his arrest in 1990.
country_links.context: The organization smuggled drugs into the United States and operated a major drug-trafficking operation in Southern California.
